input_candidates.truth_value: Grain interior: dislocation cell blocks (avg ~275 ± 50 nm at true strain 0.2); grain boundaries: intergranular M3C-type carbides (~1 μm wide × 3 μm long); deformation twins interact with carbides promoting intergranular fracture.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Grain interior remains essentially solid solution austenite (γ phase); grain boundaries and annealing twin interfaces contain intergranular M3C-type carbides. Under deformation, dislocation cell blocks develop at 0.2 true strain; twin bundles form at intermediate-to-high stress and impinge on carbides, triggering intergranular fracture.

input_candidates.truth_value: Austenitic (γ) solid solution; after tensile straining, multiple hardening stages with dislocation substructures (Taylor lattice, cells, cell blocks) and deformation twinning.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Solid solution austenite (γ phase). Under tensile deformation, exhibits multiple-stage strain hardening: first stage governed by dislocation substructures (Taylor lattice, dislocation cells, cell blocks); second stage activated by deformation twinning at high stress levels (~950 MPa true stress).

input_candidates.truth_value: Long annealing produces uniform distribution of cuboidal κ-carbides (10-20 nm) with narrow matrix channels (~2-3 nm); deformation (true strain 0.15) develops planar dislocation arrays spaced 100-300 nm, with sheared κ-carbides.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Austenite matrix with uniform distribution of intragranular cuboidal j-carbides and narrow matrix channels (~2–3 nm). Under deformation, planar dislocation substructures develop with dense dislocation arrays spaced 100–300 nm; cuboidal j-carbides are frequently sheared by dislocations.

input_candidates.prediction_value: Austenite matrix with heterogeneous distribution of intragranular nanosized j-carbides. Under deformation, planar dislocation configurations develop (Taylor lattice structures and highly dense dislocation walls, HDDWs, spaced 100–200 nm); j-carbides are crystallographically sheared by dislocations without pinning.
